repository.name: red-run
repository.description: Offensive security toolkit for Claude Code

commit.message:
Fix orchestrator: always run host discovery on shell access, including DCs

The orchestrator previously routed DCs exclusively to ad-discovery,
skipping host-level enumeration entirely. This missed additional NICs
(critical for pivoting), Hyper-V infrastructure, scheduled tasks with
stored creds, and local privilege escalation vectors.

Now: host discovery (windows-discovery/linux-discovery) runs on ALL
hosts when shell access is gained. On DCs, ad-discovery runs additionally
after host discovery completes. Sequential, not either/or.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add agent browser, double-tap dismiss, and operator approval rule

Dashboard:
- Add agent browser (b key) — lists all agent transcripts from the
  session sorted by recency, with skill/agent-type labels extracted
  from JSONL content. Agents already in the dashboard shown with ●
  marker. Enter to add, Esc/b to close.
- Double-tap dismiss (d key) — first press shows "tap d again to
  dismiss" warning in yellow, second press within 2s actually removes
  the pane. Prevents accidental dismissals.
- Fix tasks directory path derivation in dashboard.sh — sed was
  stripping a leading dash that the actual directory keeps.
- Label extraction: detect Plan/Explore agents from prompt keywords,
  extract skill names from "Load skill" patterns, fall back to prompt
  summary instead of hex agent IDs.

Orchestrator:
- Add mandatory operator approval rule before every agent spawn.
  Presented as a top-level constraint alongside "don't run scanning
  tools". No agents launch without explicit operator sign-off.

commit.message:
fix: use copy package-import-method in Docker to avoid pnpm ENOENT (#12100)

* fix: use copy package-import-method in Docker to avoid pnpm ENOENT

pnpm's default hard-link strategy fails intermittently on Docker's
overlay filesystem with "ENOENT: rename _tmp -> secp256k1". Setting
package-import-method=copy via env var (Docker-only, doesn't affect
local dev .npmrc) fixes the atomic rename race condition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add retry loop for pnpm install in Docker builds

The ENOENT rename failure persists even with package-import-method=copy
because pnpm's hoisting/linking phase still does atomic renames that
race on Docker's overlay filesystem. Since it's intermittent and all
packages are cached in the store after the first attempt, a retry with
clean node_modules is fast and reliable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: restrict swap-widget tsconfig types to prevent auto-discovery

Without an explicit `types` field, TypeScript auto-discovers all
@types/* packages hoisted to root node_modules. The deprecated stub
@types/ethereumjs-util (from hdwallet-ledger devDeps) has no .d.ts
files, causing TS2688 during Docker builds. Restricting to
["vite/client"] matches the pattern used by the root tsconfig.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: relax uprightness gate — SWIPE_MIN_HAND_UPRIGHTNESS 0.06->0.04, SWIPE_UPRIGHT_FRAMES 2->1

Uprightness delay was causing tracking window to miss the horizontal swipe
portion and only capture the downward follow-through. Chin-drop defense now
carried by x-displacement (0.08), lateral ratio (0.40), and peak velocity (0.05).

incremental adoption strategy.

- Reduce inter-batch delay from 2s to 500ms

commit.message:
Add .node-version (22) for Netlify — Next.js 16 requires Node 22+

Netlify defaults to Node 18 which cannot build Next.js 16.1.1.
This is likely why all recent deploys have been failing silently,
leaving the site stuck on an old deployment with stale chunk hashes.

commit.message:
docs: Add v1.0.0 launch readiness checklist

Complete summary of everything delivered for v1.0.0 release:

✅ Code Quality
- 46/46 unit tests passing
- 49/49 E2E tests passing (stabilized)
- Zero hardcoded secrets
- Optimized Docker builds

✅ Features Complete
- Meeting management, minutes, collaboration
- Motion tracking, action items, search
- Role-based access, multiple exports

✅ Documentation
- API docs, deployment guides, security, contributing
- Cost forecast (all scaling scenarios)
- Competitive analysis vs ChamberMate
- Complete marketing strategy package

✅ Marketing Ready
- Brand positioning + messaging framework
- Landing page copy + ad copy (Google/LinkedIn/Facebook)
- Email sequences (5-email nurture + 3-email segments)
- 4-week social media content calendar
- Testimonial + case study templates
- Referral program mechanics

✅ Business Planning
- Cost forecast: $58/month (free tier) to $10,101/month (enterprise)
- Revenue targets: $1,500-5,000 MRR by Year 1
- CAC/LTV analysis
- Market opportunity: 7,000+ chambers

✅ Working Directory
- Clean: all changes committed
- Ready: to push to main

ChamberAI is ready for immediate launch. 🚀

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Year 3 complete — self-healing, intelligence, enterprise, autonomous ops

Q1 — Self-Healing Infrastructure:
- Auto-remediation engine: 8 rules (failed login → unlock, MFA missing →
  setup, license expired → renew, etc.) with cooldown tracking and auto-execute
- Runbook engine: 10 executable IT runbooks (Exchange restart, cert rotation,
  DNS flush, Azure AD sync, etc.) with rollback capability
- Incident correlator: clusters similar user reports within 10-min windows,
  auto-creates P1 incidents at 5+ affected users, Jira integration
- Change manager: schedule/notify/execute/rollback IT changes with risk levels

Q2 — IT Intelligence Platform:
- Executive dashboard: cost per user, deflection rate, department breakdown,
  vendor spend, monthly trends
- Report builder: 6 report types (license audit, security posture, cost
  analysis, bot performance, compliance, access review) with scheduling
- Full dashboard pages for executive, operations, and reports

Q3 — Enterprise Scale:
- Multi-tenant: data isolation, plan tiers, domain-based lookup
- RBAC v2: 5 roles with spending limits, department scoping, temporary
  delegation with max duration
- Plugin system: 8-plugin catalog (1Password, Jamf, Splunk, Sentinel, Okta,
  etc.) with hook-based architecture
- Public API: 8 documented endpoints with OpenAPI 3.0 spec and rate limiting

Q4 — Autonomous Operations:
- Access reviewer: quarterly automated reviews with Slack approve/revoke
  buttons, overdue tracking, revocation execution
- Shadow IT detector: scans for unauthorized SaaS (Dropbox, Trello, Notion,
  ChatGPT) with sanctioned alternatives and user notification
- Budget forecaster: multi-quarter prediction with growth/inflation modeling,
  spend vs budget tracking, optimization identification
- Autonomous controller: deflection rate, autonomy score (0-100 with A-F
  grade), cost savings estimation ($25/ticket), 7 automation expansion
  recommendations

+8,417 lines. 30 Prisma models, 38 bot modules, 47 API routes, 20 pages.
3-year roadmap complete.

commit.message:
feat: complete Stripe payment flow with escrow and webhook notifications

Adds Stripe Checkout (card + ACH) at /api/stripe/create-checkout-session.
Buyer pay page at /pay/[id] shows full fee breakdown and Pay Securely
button. Success page at /pay/[id]/success explains next steps. Webhook
at /api/webhooks/stripe handles checkout.session.completed: updates
transaction to funded, sets funded_at and stripe_payment_intent_id,
and sends confirmation emails to both buyer and seller via Resend.

fix(estimate): use face_ids not faces for photo face lookup

commit.message:
Fix music player bugs, dark mode, and album type system

Play/queue fixes:
- Clicking a track now queues all visible tracks from that position (next/prev work)
- Play/Shuffle buttons respect active album filter and sort order
- Stale audio element event listeners now guarded so switching tracks mid-load
  no longer flips state back to paused
- canplay retry listener also guarded against stale audio elements
- 'loading' state treated as visually playing in TrackRow, MusicBar, and FullscreenPlayer

Media Session API (lock screen controls):
- Lock screen shows track title, artist, album art
- Play/pause/next/prev/seek all work from lock screen and background
- Media Session setup extracted to private setupMediaSession() method

Dark mode:
- Tailwind dark: utilities now use .dark class strategy via @custom-variant
- ThemeProvider respects system prefers-color-scheme in addition to time-of-day
- System dark mode preference changes are reactive (no 60s delay)
- suppressHydrationWarning on <html> to prevent SSR mismatch

Album type system:
- Albums can be tagged 'album' or 'draft' via admin panel
- Music page renders separate Albums and Drafts sections
- albumType uses union literal type throughout (schema, mutations, TypeScript)

Cleanup:
- formatDuration imported from @/lib/types in MusicBar (was mock-data)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add traffic channel classification (paid search, organic, social, etc.)

Replace simplistic isPaidTraffic boolean with GA4-style channel grouping.
classifyTrafficChannel() categorizes traffic into paid_search, organic_search,
paid_social, organic_social, email, referral, or direct based on UTM params,
gclid/msclkid, and referrer domain. Admin dashboard shows color-coded channel
badges next to each traffic source.
